{"key_event":"Coalition politics, Mandal, and liberalization reshape power","reasoning":"Civil society strengthened through regional-party competition, Mandal-era lower-caste mobilization, a freer press, economic liberalization, and new local-government institutions, though communal violence and insurgency limited civic freedom in parts of India. State power remained very strong through bureaucracy, courts, elections, and security forces, but Congress decline, federal constraints, reduced economic control, and failures such as Babri lowered central state dominance.","society_change":0.5,"society_power":8.3,"state_change":-0.6,"state_power":8.6}
